message:
fix(mvcc): use notify_all on WASM to prevent missed wakeups in D4 per-page wake

D4 cross-review discovered a missed-wakeup bug on WASM targets:

Problem: notify_waiters_for_page used notify_one() on the global condvar
fallback. On WASM, waiters use condvar.wait() (not thread::park), so
multiple pages with waiters compete for the single notification. If
thread B (waiting for page P2) wakes instead of thread A (waiting for
page P1), A may starve indefinitely.

Fix: Use notify_all() on WASM to ensure all waiters wake and recheck
their specific page. On native, notify_one() remains sufficient since
targeted Thread::unpark() handles the per-page wakeups.

Other review findings (no bugs):
- Double-check pattern correctly prevents missed wakeups
- Unpark permit semantics handle mutex-blocking races
- Loop structure handles spurious wakeups
- No starvation under contention

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
